encounter.reason: Addiction medicine intake evaluation for alcohol use concerns
encounter.location: Bridgetown Recovery Center, 920 SE Sandy Blvd, Portland, OR 97214

screening.name: CIWA-Ar (Clinical Institute Withdrawal Assessment for Alcohol, revised)
screening.interpretation: mild withdrawal symptoms present at time of evaluation

medications.name: naltrexone 50 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one 50 mg tablet by mouth once daily
medications.reason: alcohol use disorder — craving reduction and relapse prevention

medications.name: thiamine 100 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one 100 mg tablet by mouth once daily
medications.reason: nutritional support given chronic heavy alcohol use and risk of thiamine deficiency

medications.name: quetiapine 50 mg oral tablet
medications.reason: bipolar II disorder — sleep and mood stabilization

document_references.description: Comprehensive new-patient intake evaluation for alcohol use concerns at Bridgetown Recovery Center. Documents detailed alcohol use history, psychiatric history, AUDIT and CIWA-Ar screening scores, physical examination findings, lab results, new diagnosis of alcohol use disorder moderate severity, initiation of naltrexone and thiamine, enrollment in 12-week IOP, and care coordination with psychiatrist Dr. Okafor.

diagnostic_reports.type: comprehensive metabolic panel (CMP) with liver enzymes
diagnostic_reports.description: CMP ordered as part of addiction medicine intake workup. AST and ALT are reported as individual observations (elevated). The remainder of the CMP was within normal limits.
CBC ordered as part of intake workup. Result was within normal limits.
Urine drug screen ordered to confirm absence of illicit substance use prior to naltrexone initiation. Result negative for illicit substances.
Ordered to evaluate for alcohol-related liver disease given elevated liver enzymes and history of chronic heavy alcohol use. Pending scheduling at time of visit.
